source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/TimeSeries/ARAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D5 LOOSE smoke oracle (self-computed from the ported C++ core, NOT yet emitter-verified; D6 tightens to exact). ARAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over an AR(1) AutoRegressive model, then builds the posterior forecast ensemble (mode/mean/credible band) over the observed range plus forecasting_time_steps. training_time_steps is pinned to 15 (the model default of max(30, floor(0.8*n)) exceeds this 20-point series and would be invalid). curve_length = 20 observed + 3 forecast = 23. mode_curve[0] is the deterministic first observed value. Values reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python; thinning_interval pinned to 1. Short run (iterations 100, output_length 400). Tolerances loose (rel 1e-5); the exact seeded forecast values are not C#-reproducible (a documented P4-class MersenneTwister-seed concern). D6 oracle-exemption: this analysis target has NO C# numeric oracle (D1/D2 shipped the per-family analyses as STRUCTURAL oracles only; the seeded forecast trajectory is not C#-reproducible -- documented P4-class MersenneTwister-seed concern) and the emitter has no driver for it, so the fixture carries "oracle_skip": true: the dev-only dotnet gate (verify_oracles.py) SKIPS these assertions while the shipped C++/R/Python harnesses still check the self-computed values. See docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md.
